entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A staple legume grown in sub-Saharan Africa

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Region where cowpea is a staple food and Bt-cowpea was developed

entities.name: MARUCA VITRATA
entities.description: The bean pod borer insect pest that causes significant yield loss in cowpea

entities.name: YIELD LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in seed production caused by pest infestation

entities.name: CRY1AB GENE
entities.description: A gene from Bacillus thuringiensis that provides resistance to Maruca vitrata

entities.name: BACILLUS THURINGIENSIS
entities.description: The soil bacterium from which the cry1Ab gene is derived

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to withstand attack by Maruca vitrata

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: Country where Bt-cowpea efficacy was evaluated

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: Country where Bt-cowpea efficacy was evaluated

entities.name: GHANA
entities.description: Country where Bt-cowpea efficacy was evaluated

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: Region encompassing Nigeria, Burkina Faso, and Ghana

entities.name: CONFINED FIELD TRIALS
entities.description: Controlled field experiments used to evaluate the efficacy of Bt-cowpea

entities.name: INSECT-PROOF NETTING
entities.description: Physical barrier used in field trials to control insect access

entities.name: BUD FORMATION AND FLOWERING PERIODS
entities.description: Specific growth stages of cowpea during which infestation occurred

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of seeds produced by the cowpea plant

entities.name: INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A strategy combining Bt-cowpea with minimal insecticide treatment

entities.name: INSECTICIDE TREATMENT
entities.description: Chemical application used to protect against other insects

entities.name: INSECT RESISTANCE MANAGEMENT PLAN
entities.description: A plan to prevent pests from developing resistance to the Bt toxin

entities.name: HIGH-DOSE REFUGE STRATEGY
entities.description: A strategy using non-Bt cowpea to maintain a susceptible population of pests

entities.name: TWO-TOXIN COWPEA PYRAMID
entities.description: A future genetically engineered cowpea variety containing two toxins
